bindings.quest_action: gather barricade materials for a last stand at the watchtower
bindings.quest_focus: the old watchtower that must hold long enough for evacuation
bindings.quest_stakes: families being overrun during a chaotic retreat
bindings.quest_threat: an advancing warband that will crush the settlement without a delay
bindings.quest_time_pressure: every hour without barricades is an hour closer to losing everyone

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I need to speak with you. What I'm about to say may sound reckless, but I've thought it through a hundred times and my answer is always the same.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} have been pushing closer to {location} every night. The outer farms are already abandoned. The council wants to pull everyone behind the walls and wait it out, but I've seen what waiting gets you: slow starvation and a grave nobody remembers. I'm going to take a stand at {quest_focus} {location_hint}. Hold the line there so the families have time to evacuate properly. I need someone to {quest_action} before I go.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know I probably won't come back. But if even one child makes it out because I bought them an extra hour, that's a trade I'll make gladly.

playerResponses.text: That's the bravest thing I've heard in a long time. I'll get what you need.
You're throwing your life away, but I'll help you do it right.
There has to be a better plan than a suicide stand.

bindings.quest_action: find materials to create a permanent seal for the mine shaft
bindings.quest_focus: a collapsed mine leaking poison into the river
bindings.quest_stakes: children continuing to drink contaminated water
bindings.quest_threat: toxic fumes in the mine that will kill whoever seals it
bindings.quest_origin: enemy refuse dumped into the water source upstream

dialogueChunks.intro: You carry yourself like someone who understands duty, {player_name}. I hope I'm reading you right.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: There's a sickness spreading through the wells in {location}. The alchemist says it's not natural: something is poisoning the water source upstream. {target_npc} traced it to {quest_focus} {location_hint} where {enemy_type} have been dumping foul refuse into the river. Someone needs to go in and seal the source, but {quest_threat} after prolonged exposure. I've volunteered. All I need is {quest_item} to create a seal strong enough to last.
dialogueChunks.outro: The fumes will take me eventually. I've accepted that. But the water will run clean for generations. That matters more than one old soldier.

playerResponses.text: Let me help you gather what you need. Your sacrifice won't be wasted.
I respect your resolve. Tell me where to find the materials.
Surely there's a way to seal it without killing yourself in the process.

bindings.quest_action: fetch explosives to rig the bridge supports
bindings.quest_focus: the only bridge crossing for three days' travel
bindings.quest_stakes: every settlement on this side of the river being overrun
bindings.quest_threat: a massive warband that will cross the bridge and destroy everything
bindings.quest_time_pressure: the warband arriving before the bridge can be collapsed

dialogueChunks.intro: I've written my letters. Said my goodbyes in my own way. Now there's just one thing left to do, and I could use your help with part of it, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} at {location} is the only crossing for three days' travel in either direction. {target_npc} confirmed that a massive {enemy_type} warband is marching this way. If they cross that bridge, {quest_stakes}. I'm going to collapse the bridge once everyone is across, but I need to be on the far side when it falls to make sure it's done right. I need {quest_item} to rig the supports. Will you fetch it?
dialogueChunks.outro: Someone has to be the one who stays behind. I've lived long enough. The young ones haven't.

playerResponses.text: I'll bring you everything you need. You have my word.
This is a hard plan, but it's the right one. I'll help.
There must be another way to stop them without losing you.
